subject: Chemistry
chapter: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes; Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers

questions.question: A compound containing an OH group directly attached to an aromatic ring carbon is classified as a ______.
questions.explanation: A phenol contains a hydroxyl group directly attached to an aromatic ring carbon.

questions.question: CH2=CHCH2OH is classified as an ______.
questions.explanation: The OH-bearing carbon is directly adjacent to a carbon-carbon double bond, so the compound is an allylic alcohol.

questions.question: The common name of benzene-1,2-diol is ______.
questions.explanation: Benzene-1,2-diol is commonly known as catechol.

questions.question: The common name of benzene-1,3-diol is ______.
questions.explanation: Benzene-1,3-diol is commonly known as resorcinol.

questions.question: The common name of benzene-1,4-diol is ______.
questions.explanation: Benzene-1,4-diol is commonly known as hydroquinone.

questions.question: The IUPAC name of CH3OCH3 is ______.
questions.explanation: CH3OCH3 is named methoxymethane according to the alkoxyalkane system.

questions.question: The IUPAC name of CH3OCH2CH3 is ______.
questions.explanation: The ethane chain is selected as the parent and OCH3 is named as a methoxy substituent.

questions.question: The C=O functional group present in aldehydes and ketones is called the ______ group.
questions.explanation: A carbon atom double-bonded to oxygen forms the carbonyl group.

questions.question: In the conversion of a carbonyl compound into an alcohol, LiAlH4 is classified as a ______.
questions.explanation: LiAlH4 acts as a reducing agent during the conversion of carbonyl compounds into alcohols.

questions.question: An organomagnesium compound represented by RMgX belongs to the ______ reagent family.
questions.explanation: Organomagnesium compounds represented by RMgX are called Grignard reagents.

questions.question: The acid-catalysed addition of water across a carbon-carbon double bond is called ______.
questions.explanation: Hydration involves the addition of water across the double bond of an alkene.

questions.question: Hydroboration-oxidation of an unsymmetrical alkene follows ______ orientation.
questions.explanation: Hydroboration-oxidation places the hydroxyl group on the less substituted carbon and follows anti-Markovnikov orientation.

questions.question: The intermolecular attraction mainly responsible for the high boiling points of alcohols and phenols is ______.
questions.explanation: Alcohols and phenols form intermolecular hydrogen bonds through their hydroxyl groups.

questions.question: The hydrocarbon used as the industrial starting material for preparing phenol by the cumene process is ______.
questions.explanation: Cumene is the hydrocarbon starting material used in the industrial cumene process for phenol.

questions.question: An alcohol whose OH-bearing carbon is bonded to three other carbon atoms is classified as a ______.
questions.explanation: In a tertiary alcohol, the carbon bearing the hydroxyl group is attached to three carbon atoms.

questions.question: The hybridisation of the oxygen atom in a simple alcohol is ______.
questions.explanation: The oxygen atom of an alcohol has two sigma bonds and two lone pairs and is treated as sp3 hybridised.

questions.question: The IUPAC name of CH3CH2Cl is ______.
questions.explanation: CH3CH2Cl contains a two-carbon ethane chain with a chlorine substituent and is named chloroethane.

questions.question: CH2=CHCl is classified as a ______.
questions.explanation: In CH2=CHCl, chlorine is directly attached to an sp2 carbon of a carbon-carbon double bond, making it a vinylic halide.

questions.question: C6H5CH2Cl is classified as a ______.
questions.explanation: The chlorine-bearing sp3 carbon is directly adjacent to a benzene ring, making the compound a benzylic halide.

questions.question: C6H5Cl is classified as an ______.
questions.explanation: In C6H5Cl, chlorine is directly attached to an aromatic ring carbon, so it is an aryl halide.

questions.question: Due to the electronegativity difference between carbon and halogen, the C-X bond in a haloalkane is classified as ______.
questions.explanation: The halogen attracts the shared electron pair more strongly than carbon, making the C-X bond polar covalent.

questions.question: The reagent preferred for converting alcohols into alkyl chlorides because its by-products escape as gases is ______.
questions.explanation: Thionyl chloride, SOCl2, produces gaseous SO2 and HCl by-products, simplifying purification.

questions.question: The named reaction used to prepare alkyl fluorides from alkyl chlorides or bromides is the ______.
questions.explanation: The Swarts reaction replaces chlorine or bromine in an alkyl halide with fluorine.

questions.question: The conversion of an alkyl chloride or bromide into an alkyl iodide using NaI in dry acetone is called the ______.
questions.explanation: The Finkelstein reaction uses NaI in dry acetone to prepare alkyl iodides.

questions.question: Conversion of a benzene diazonium salt into an aryl chloride or bromide using a Cu(I) salt is called the ______.
questions.explanation: The Sandmeyer reaction replaces the diazonium group with chlorine or bromine using a copper(I) salt.

questions.question: Coupling of two alkyl halide molecules using sodium in dry ether is called the ______.
questions.explanation: The Wurtz reaction couples alkyl halides using sodium in dry ether.

questions.question: An electron-pair donor that attacks an electron-deficient centre is called a ______.
questions.explanation: A nucleophile donates an electron pair to an electron-deficient centre during a reaction.

questions.question: The organic product class formed by carbon-end attack when an alkyl halide reacts with KCN is a ______.
questions.explanation: KCN favours carbon-end attack of the cyanide group, producing a nitrile.

questions.question: The organic product class formed by nitrogen-end attack when an alkyl halide reacts with AgCN is an ______.
questions.explanation: AgCN favours nitrogen-end attack and forms an isocyanide, also called an isonitrile.

questions.question: A one-step nucleophilic substitution mechanism involving backside attack is designated ______.
questions.explanation: SN2 is a concerted one-step substitution mechanism involving backside nucleophilic attack.

questions.question: A nucleophilic substitution mechanism proceeding through a planar carbocation intermediate is designated ______.
questions.explanation: SN1 substitution proceeds stepwise through a planar carbocation intermediate.

questions.question: A carbon atom bonded to four different groups is called a ______.
questions.explanation: A carbon bonded to four different groups is a chiral or asymmetric carbon.

questions.question: A pair of non-superimposable mirror-image stereoisomers are called ______.
questions.explanation: Enantiomers are stereoisomers that are non-superimposable mirror images of each other.

questions.question: The stereochemical change caused by backside attack in an SN2 reaction is called ______.
questions.explanation: Backside attack in an SN2 reaction causes inversion of configuration.

questions.question: Preservation of the original spatial arrangement around a stereogenic carbon is called ______.
questions.explanation: Retention describes preservation of the original configuration around a stereogenic centre.

questions.question: Formation of a mixture through nucleophilic attack on both sides of a planar carbocation is called ______.
questions.explanation: Attack on both sides of a planar carbocation leads to racemisation.

questions.question: Removal of a beta-hydrogen and a halogen from adjacent carbon atoms is called ______.
questions.explanation: Beta-elimination removes hydrogen from a beta-carbon and halogen from the adjacent alpha-carbon.

questions.question: The rule predicting the more substituted alkene as the major product of beta-elimination is the ______.
questions.explanation: Zaitsev's rule predicts that the more substituted alkene is generally the major elimination product.

questions.question: DMSO belongs to the ______ solvent class.
questions.explanation: DMSO is a polar aprotic solvent and is commonly favourable for SN2 reactions.

questions.question: The progressive increase in DDT concentration at successive trophic levels is called ______.
questions.explanation: Biomagnification is the increase in concentration of persistent substances such as DDT along a food chain.

questions.question: Why is the oxygen atom in a simple alcohol treated as sp3 hybridised?
options: It has one sigma bond and three lone pairs
It has two sigma bonds and two lone pairs
It has one double bond and one lone pair
It has three sigma bonds and no lone pair
It has two pi bonds and two lone pairs
questions.explanation: Alcohol oxygen has two sigma bonds and two lone pairs, giving four electron regions and sp3 hybridisation.

questions.question: Reduction of an aldehyde generally forms which class of alcohol?
questions.explanation: Aldehydes are generally reduced to primary alcohols.

questions.question: Reduction of a ketone generally produces which class of alcohol?
questions.explanation: Reduction of the carbonyl group of a ketone produces a secondary alcohol.

questions.question: Acid-catalysed hydration of propene mainly produces:
questions.explanation: Acid-catalysed hydration follows Markovnikov addition, giving propan-2-ol as the major product.

questions.question: Hydroboration-oxidation of propene mainly produces:
questions.explanation: Hydroboration-oxidation follows anti-Markovnikov orientation and produces propan-1-ol.

questions.question: Why is the C-O bond in phenol shorter than the C-O bond in many alcohols?
options: Phenol contains an ionic C-O bond
Resonance gives the C-O bond partial double-bond character
The oxygen atom has no lone pair
The benzene ring removes the oxygen atom's electrons completely
Phenol contains a carbon-oxygen triple bond
questions.explanation: Resonance involving the oxygen lone pair gives the C-O bond in phenol partial double-bond character.

questions.question: In IUPAC nomenclature of an unsymmetrical ether, the smaller alkyl group attached through oxygen is generally named as:
questions.explanation: Ethers are named as alkoxyalkanes, with the smaller group generally represented as the alkoxy substituent.

questions.question: In the IUPAC nomenclature of a cyclic alcohol containing one OH group, the OH-bearing carbon is assigned:
options: The locant of the nearest substituent
questions.explanation: In a cyclic alcohol, the carbon bearing the principal OH group is assigned carbon number 1.

questions.question: A Grignard reagent reacts with methanal followed by hydrolysis. Which class of alcohol is obtained?
questions.explanation: Grignard reagents react with methanal followed by hydrolysis to produce primary alcohols.

questions.question: A Grignard reagent reacts with a ketone followed by hydrolysis. Which class of alcohol is formed?
questions.explanation: Addition of a Grignard reagent to a ketone followed by hydrolysis produces a tertiary alcohol.

questions.question: Which conditions are used to convert chlorobenzene into sodium phenoxide before acidification to phenol?
options: Aqueous NaOH at 623 K and 320 atm
questions.explanation: Chlorobenzene is heated with aqueous NaOH at about 623 K and 300 atm to form sodium phenoxide, which gives phenol on acidification.

questions.question: Which compound is formed along with phenol in the cumene process?
questions.explanation: Acidic decomposition in the cumene process produces phenol and acetone.

questions.question: Why does the carbon atom of a C-X bond in a haloalkane carry a partial positive charge?
options: Halogen is more electronegative than carbon
Carbon is more electronegative than halogen
Halogen donates electrons completely to carbon
questions.explanation: The more electronegative halogen attracts the shared electron pair, leaving carbon partially positive.

questions.question: When an alcohol reacts with a hydrogen halide, HX, the OH group is ultimately replaced by:
questions.explanation: Hydrogen halides convert alcohols into haloalkanes by replacing the hydroxyl group with a halogen.

questions.question: Which phosphorus reagent converts alcohols into alkyl chlorides while forming phosphorous acid as a by-product?
questions.explanation: PCl3 converts alcohols into alkyl chlorides and forms phosphorous acid.

questions.question: Which reagent converts an alcohol into an alkyl chloride with POCl3 and HCl as by-products?
questions.explanation: PCl5 converts alcohols into alkyl chlorides, producing POCl3 and HCl.

questions.question: Why is SOCl2 often preferred for preparing alkyl chlorides from alcohols?
questions.explanation: SO2 and HCl formed during the reaction escape as gases, leaving the alkyl chloride relatively pure.

questions.question: What mainly drives the Finkelstein reaction forward in dry acetone?
questions.explanation: NaCl or NaBr precipitates from dry acetone, shifting the halogen-exchange reaction forward.

questions.question: The boiling points of homologous haloalkanes generally increase with increasing:
questions.explanation: Increasing molecular mass generally strengthens dispersion forces and raises the boiling point.

questions.question: Why are haloalkanes generally only slightly soluble in water?
options: Haloalkane-water attractions cannot compensate for breaking water hydrogen bonds
Haloalkanes form very strong hydrogen bonds with water
Water converts every haloalkane into a Grignard reagent
questions.explanation: The energy released by haloalkane-water attractions is insufficient to compensate for breaking hydrogen bonds between water molecules.

questions.question: Which of the following acts as a strong nucleophile in nucleophilic substitution reactions?
questions.explanation: OH− is a negatively charged, electron-rich species and acts as a strong nucleophile.

questions.question: Which substrate most strongly favours an SN1 reaction?
questions.explanation: Tertiary haloalkanes favour SN1 reactions because they form relatively stable tertiary carbocations.

questions.question: Which combination most strongly favours an SN2 reaction?
options: Methyl halide and polar aprotic solvent
Tertiary haloalkane and polar protic solvent
questions.explanation: Low steric hindrance in methyl halides and a polar aprotic solvent strongly favour the SN2 mechanism.

questions.question: What is the major difference in the action of aqueous KOH and alcoholic KOH on haloalkanes?
options: Aqueous KOH favours substitution while alcoholic KOH favours elimination
Aqueous KOH favours elimination while alcoholic KOH favours substitution
questions.explanation: Aqueous KOH generally favours nucleophilic substitution, whereas alcoholic KOH favours beta-elimination.

questions.question: Why are Grignard reagents highly reactive?
options: The C-Mg bond is strongly polar and carbon has carbanion-like character
The C-Mg bond is completely non-polar
They contain a carbon-carbon triple bond
questions.explanation: The strongly polar C-Mg bond gives carbon substantial negative character, making Grignard reagents highly reactive.

questions.question: Why is dry ether used while preparing and handling a Grignard reagent?
options: Ether oxygen coordinates with magnesium and stabilises the reagent
Ether supplies water to the reagent
Ether acts as a strong oxidising agent
questions.explanation: Lone pairs on ether oxygen coordinate with magnesium and help stabilise the Grignard reagent.

questions.question: Why must water be excluded from a Grignard reaction?
options: Water protonates the reactive carbon and destroys the Grignard reagent
Water converts the reagent into an alkyl fluoride
Water increases the C-Mg bond polarity indefinitely
questions.explanation: Grignard reagents react rapidly with water; the reactive carbon is protonated and the reagent is destroyed.

questions.question: Why are haloarenes less reactive than haloalkanes towards nucleophilic substitution?
options: Resonance gives the C-X bond partial double-bond character
The C-X bond becomes completely ionic
The halogen atom loses all lone pairs
The aromatic ring contains only single bonds
questions.explanation: Resonance gives the carbon-halogen bond in haloarenes partial double-bond character, making it shorter and stronger.

questions.question: How does a nitro group at the ortho or para position affect nucleophilic substitution of a haloarene?
options: It increases reactivity by stabilising the negatively charged intermediate
It converts the halogen into a nucleophile
It removes aromaticity before the reaction begins
It converts the haloarene into a Grignard reagent
questions.explanation: An ortho or para nitro group withdraws electron density and stabilises the negatively charged intermediate formed during nucleophilic substitution.

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes halogens during electrophilic substitution of haloarenes?
options: They are deactivating but ortho-para directing
They are activating and meta directing
They are deactivating and meta directing
They are activating but only para directing
They have no effect on orientation
questions.explanation: Halogens deactivate the aromatic ring through the inductive effect but direct incoming electrophiles to ortho and para positions through resonance.
